exercises.title: Identify State and Behavior
exercises.description: Define a simple BankAccount class with state (attributes) and behavior (methods) — no __init__ yet. Add attributes directly to instances and call their methods.
exercises.starterCode:
class BankAccount:
    """A simple bank account."""

    def get_info(self):
        """Print account info."""
        # TODO: Print: "Owner: {owner}, Balance: ${balance:.2f}"
        pass

    def is_positive(self):
        """Return True if balance > 0."""
        # TODO: return True if self.balance > 0
        pass


# TODO: Create an account and set these attributes directly:
# account.owner = 'Alice'
# account.balance = 500.0

# TODO: Call get_info()

# TODO: Print the result of is_positive()
hints: account.owner = 'Alice' sets an attribute on the instance
Inside get_info, use self.owner and self.balance

exercises.title: Class as Blueprint
exercises.description: Create a Dog class with a class attribute 'species'. Create three different dog instances, set their names and breeds directly, and verify they all share the same species.
exercises.starterCode:
class Dog:
    # TODO: Add class attribute: species = 'Canis lupus familiaris'
    pass


# TODO: Create three Dog instances: fido, rex, buddy

# TODO: Set name and breed on each:
# fido: name='Fido', breed='Labrador'
# rex: name='Rex', breed='German Shepherd'
# buddy: name='Buddy', breed='Golden Retriever'

# TODO: Print each dog's name, breed, and species
# Format: "Fido (Labrador) - Species: Canis lupus familiaris"

# TODO: Verify all dogs have the same species using 'is'
# Print: "Same species: True"
exercises.expectedOutput:
Fido (Labrador) - Species: Canis lupus familiaris
Rex (German Shepherd) - Species: Canis lupus familiaris
Buddy (Golden Retriever) - Species: Canis lupus familiaris
Same species: True
hints: Access species via fido.species or Dog.species
fido.species is rex.species is True because it's the same string object

exercises.title: Procedural to OOP Conversion
exercises.description: Convert the procedural student grade functions below into a Student class where the data and functions live together.

warmup.title: Quick Recall: Everything is an Object
warmup.description: Python's built-in types are already classes. What does this code print?
warmup.starterCode:
name = "Alice"
grades = [92, 85, 78]

print(type(name))
print(type(grades))
print(name.upper())
print(grades.count(85))
